text.headline: Kehan Dong - Founder Chronicle
text.text: A timeline of entrepreneurship, education, investing, and founder support.

text.headline: The Code of Love (NGO) - Founder and President
text.text: Founded The Code of Love while in high school in Shanghai. Grew from a small volunteer initiative into a long-running nonprofit focused on reducing educational inequality. Organized 1,700+ volunteers and delivered educational programs to more than 6,500 underprivileged students across five countries. Built libraries, supported rural teaching programs, and connected students from low-income backgrounds with long-term sponsorships so they can complete their education through university. Realized that while traditional charity matters, technology and innovation-driven education create far greater leverage, leading to a shift toward science, technology, and entrepreneurship education.

text.headline: University of California - Dropped Out
text.text: Attended University of California before dropping out to pursue entrepreneurship full-time.

text.headline: Inner Journeys - Founder and CEO
text.text: Started Inner Journeys while still in university. Grew into a full-time company, went through multiple rounds of funding, and scaled the product through several iterations. The company was eventually acquired in 2020. This journey shaped much of the thinking about building from zero, understanding users, and navigating the early chaos of a startup. Led to Forbes 30 Under 30 recognition and gave a deep appreciation for how young founders learn and grow.

text.headline: Y Combinator China - Associate, Founding Team
text.text: Joined the founding team of YC China in early 2019, selected as one of the youngest members to explore YC’s potential second global headquarters. Worked with Sam Altman and the global YC network to help design a YC-style model in China. Focused on investment evaluation, founder support, and program design. Explored how to adapt YC’s principles to a new market and build a long-term ecosystem for young founders. Even though the initiative ended early as Sam focused on OpenAI, this experience shaped thinking about early-stage founders, scalable talent discovery, and what it takes to build a world-class entrepreneurship platform.

text.headline: MiraclePlus - Investment & Operations, Founding Team
text.text: Core member of the founding team at MiraclePlus (formerly Y Combinator China). Worked on investment, operations, and founder support across multiple batches. Over six years, evaluated 160,000+ applications, interviewed thousands of founders, and participated in 600+ early-stage investments across AI, biotech, robotics, hard tech, and consumer. Built a 7,000+ member intern sourcing engine and developed a scalable model for discovering high-potential young founders. This experience shaped a long-term curiosity around how to help young people build meaningful companies earlier in life.

text.headline: Founder & Angel Investor - Self-employed
text.text: Invested in 30 young founders across AI, biotech, robotics, and consumer after traveling across 40 top U.S. universities. Currently investing as a personal angel and working closely with student founders, researchers, and early teams. Over the past six years, made 650+ early-stage investments and built the largest student founder pipelines in China. Researching the next chapter — a new type of entrepreneurship platform for young builders combining real-world projects, long-term mentorship, AI-native tools, and community.
